violations.finding: The command embeds actual workflow shell logic in bash blocks across Steps 1-6, 9, 11, 13, and the Test Folder Initialization section, including venv/script execution, mkdir, cp, if/elif conditionals, find, jq parsing, git operations, checkpoint mutation, and test-folder initialization. This is implementation logic that executes real operations, not examples. Examples are separately labeled or contained in placeholder shells.
violations.recommendation: Extract orchestration logic into ~/.claude/scripts/test-orchestrator.sh or similar script; reduce test.md steps to concise delegation calls and high-level rules only.

violations.finding: The command embeds large runtime context schemas (Steps 7, 10), expected JSON output schemas (Steps 8, 12), decision-tree pseudocode (Steps 9, 13), and a full subagent prompt template (Step 12, lines 393-421). These are runtime prototypes and documentation bloat that are loaded on every invocation but add no actionable rules.
violations.recommendation: Reduce to concise delegation instructions (e.g., 'Delegate to test-validator subagent with context from tests/reports/'); move JSON schemas to script files or template references.

violations.finding: Steps 14-16 embed full verbatim sample output templates: a ~70-line markdown failure report template (Step 14), a 5-option interactive menu block with per-option prose descriptions (Step 15), and a 125-line completion report markdown template (Step 16). These are runtime output prototypes, not rules, and waste context tokens on every agent invocation.
violations.recommendation: Remove embedded sample output templates. Replace with concise output requirements (1-3 lines each). Move completion report template to a separate template file generated by a script.

violations.finding: Trailing sections — Test Folder Initialization (with duplicate bash blocks), validator list (10 entries), Safety Features, Quality Standards, Helper Scripts Used, Integration with Other Commands, and Usage — duplicate information already present in the step-by-step workflow and add non-actionable descriptive prose beyond concise command instructions.
violations.recommendation: Remove or consolidate into a 3-5 line reference block listing key scripts and their purposes.

violations.finding: Phase 6 embeds a full 37-line markdown runtime report template (section headers, placeholder fields, URL lists, issue tracking, recommendations, search path log). This is a runtime output format specification embedded as documentation bloat rather than a concise reporting instruction.
violations.recommendation: Replace with a concise reporting instruction: 'Synthesize findings into a structured report covering: search goal, key findings with URLs, issues encountered, and recommendations.' Move any formal template to a script or separate template file.

violations.finding: Phase 6 embeds a full 45-line markdown runtime report template (executive summary, main findings with evidence/sources/confidence, detailed analysis, contrarian viewpoints, information gaps, sources used, research metadata, recommendations). This is a runtime output prototype embedded in the command file, loaded on every invocation.
violations.recommendation: Replace with a concise reporting instruction: 'Compile a report covering: executive summary, 5-7 key findings with evidence and sources, contrarian viewpoints, information gaps, and source list.' Move any formal template to a separate template file.

violations.finding: The command file is a verbose integration guide: the 'Playwright is PRIMARY' statement is repeated three times (lines 29-30, 96-100, 127-128); three separate per-command usage examples are shown (deep-search, site-navigate, research-deep); a Troubleshooting section with 3 issue/solution pairs; a Technical Details section; and a 'Ready to Use!' closing section with marketing language. The file's total length (134 lines) exceeds what is needed for a reference command file.
violations.recommendation: Reduce to: one-line purpose, one-line strategy (WebSearch first, then Playwright), a single usage example, and one-line technical reference (install command). Remove troubleshooting prose (it belongs in docs/), repeated statements, and marketing closing.

standards_passed: no-hardcoded-values (all 5 files — fswatch.md env var block is configuration documentation, not hardcoded logic)
use-source-venv (test.md:36 uses source venv before python3; no bare python3 calls in other files)
integer-step-numbering (all 5 files — all steps use integer numbering)
english-only (all 5 files — no Chinese or non-English characters found)
merge-similar-scripts (no duplicate-purpose scripts identified across these files)
parameterized-scripts (not applicable — these are .md command files, not scripts)

dangling-references (all referenced scripts verified to exist: hooks/fswatch-manager.sh, hooks/checkpoint.sh, scripts/todo/test.py, scripts/analyze-git-edge-cases.sh, scripts/cleanup-tests-folder.sh, scripts/migrate-test-to-tests.sh)
merge-conflicts (no merge conflict markers found in any file)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex confirmed no false positives in the 3 draft violations. It identified 4 additional issues: (1) inline bash location range in test.md was too narrow (missed lines 495-511 and 782-792); (2) concise-doc violation in test.md should also cover lines 185-527 (JSON schemas, decision trees, subagent prompt templates before Step 14); (3) trailing bloat in test.md starts at line 778 not 855; (4) deep-search.md, research-deep.md, and playwright-helper.md were incorrectly marked clean — all three contain embedded runtime report templates or verbose integration guide content violating Standard 8.

findings.issue: test.md no-inline-code location range 35-387 missed additional inline bash blocks at lines 495-511 and 782-792
findings.proposed_fix: Expand location to commands/test.md:35-183,295-307,370-387,495-511,782-792 with updated finding text
findings.rationale: Location was accurate but incomplete; codex correctly identified additional bash blocks with inline logic outside the stated range.

findings.issue: concise-doc draft #2 starting at line 533 missed earlier JSON schemas, decision trees, and subagent prompt templates in lines 185-527
findings.proposed_fix: Expand to commands/test.md:185-527 covering runtime context schemas, JSON output schemas, decision-tree pseudocode, and subagent prompt template
findings.rationale: The violation was real and larger than drafted; the earlier sections add equal or greater documentation bloat.

findings.issue: Location bug in draft #3: trailing bloat starts at line 778, not 855
findings.rationale: Verified by reading — Safety Features section starts at ~809, but the duplicate Test Folder Initialization bash blocks and validator list start at 778.

findings.issue: deep-search.md Phase 6 report template (lines 64-101) not flagged
findings.proposed_fix: Add finding: concise-documentation, critical, commands/deep-search.md:64-101, Phase 6 embeds a full markdown runtime report template
findings.rationale: Verified: lines 64-101 are a 37-line embedded markdown output template — same pattern as the test.md violations.

findings.issue: research-deep.md Phase 6 report template (lines 53-97) not flagged
findings.proposed_fix: Add finding: concise-documentation, critical, commands/research-deep.md:53-97, Phase 6 embeds a full markdown runtime report template
findings.rationale: Verified: lines 53-97 are a 45-line embedded markdown output template.

findings.issue: playwright-helper.md verbose guide (entire file) not flagged
findings.proposed_fix: Add finding: concise-documentation, critical, commands/playwright-helper.md:6-134, verbose integration guide with repeated statements, multiple examples, troubleshooting section, and marketing language
findings.rationale: Verified: file contains three repetitions of 'Playwright is PRIMARY', three per-command usage examples, troubleshooting Q&A pairs, and closing marketing prose. Per Standard 8, command files should contain purpose + usage + rules + one example, not 134-line guides.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 6 codex PROPOSED_FIX items were applied. Draft violations expanded from 3 to 7: test.md no-inline-code location corrected; test.md concise-doc split into two separate findings covering 185-527 and 533-774; test.md trailing bloat location corrected to 778-890; three new findings added for deep-search.md, research-deep.md, and playwright-helper.md.
